message:
test(fcp-raptorq,fcp-sdk): add decoder tests and minor fixes

- decode.rs: add batch of new decoder tests covering edge cases
- envelope.rs: minor fix
- oti.rs: additional OTI test cases
- streaming.rs: minor streaming module adjustments
- .beads/issues.jsonl: update issue statuses

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1095,7 +1095,7 @@
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-m6u7.7","title":"fcp.sharepoint (via fcp.microsoft365): Sites/Docs/Search","description":"# fcp.sharepoint (via fcp.microsoft365): Sites/Docs/Search

## Goal
Implement SharePoint site/document operations via Microsoft Graph:
- enumerate sites/drives/lists
- read/write documents (Dangerous)
- search (potentially sensitive; policy-gated)
- permissions and sharing links (Dangerous)

## FCP2 Requirements
- Capability gated.
- Single-zone binding.
- Default deny.
- Writes emit receipts/audit and fail closed.

## NetworkConstraints
- Default deny; Graph + required OAuth endpoints as declared in `flywheel_connectors-m6u7.1`.

## State model
- Stateless for simple reads.
- Stateful where webhook subscriptions or delta tokens are used (singleton-writer lease).

## Test strategy
- Mock-only tests: large file streaming bounds, throttling, retry semantics, redaction.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Typed schemas + validation.
- Error taxonomy mapping + no secret/PII leakage.
- Structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-m6u7.8","title":"m365.sync: Delta Tokens + Webhook Subscriptions","description":"# m365.sync: Delta Tokens + Webhook Subscriptions

## Goal
Support incremental sync and webhook subscriptions.

## Requirements
- Persist delta tokens/subscription state in mesh state objects.
- Singleton-writer lease for cursor advancement and renewal.
- Connector does not expose inbound ports; `fcp-host` owns ingress.

## Acceptance Criteria
- The functionality described in this bead is implemented with typed schemas + validation (no unstructured “stringly” interfaces).
- Errors map to the FCP error taxonomy with correct retry semantics and no secret/PII leakage.
- Mock-only unit/integration tests cover success paths and common failures and emit structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.
","notes":"2026-03-03 WhiteBay: Validating delta-token + webhook subscription implementation in fcp-microsoft365 for closure evidence (check/clippy/test via rch).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"WhiteBay","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T08:45:43.953509186Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-03-03T02:20:23.495946Z","closed_at":"2026-03-03T02:20:23.495926Z","close_reason":"Completed: added persisted m365_sync_state (delta tokens + subscription metadata) and m365_sync_lease singleton-writer fencing in connectors/microsoft365/src/connector.rs; handshake now captures zone_dir and sync/subscription ops require it. Added tests for zone_dir enforcement, persisted delta-token reuse, subscription create/delete state persistence, and lease fencing; updated integration handshake helper to pass unique zone_dir. Validation via rch exec: cargo check -p fcp-microsoft365 --all-targets (pass), cargo clippy -p fcp-microsoft365 --all-targets -- -D warnings (pass), cargo test -p fcp-microsoft365 (123 unit + 47 integration pass). Workspace check pass; workspace clippy currently fails on unrelated pre-existing gmail clone_on_copy warning.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["connectors","fcp2"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-m6u7.8","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.33","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-m6u7.8","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-m6u7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-m6u7.8","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-m6u7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-m6u7.9","title":"TEST: Microsoft365 Connector Unit/Integration Tests (mock-only)","description":"# TEST: Microsoft365 Connector Unit/Integration Tests (mock-only)


## Goal
Validate Microsoft365 connector via mock-only tests (Graph APIs and planned operation slices) with deterministic behavior.

## Must cover
- Manifest determinism + interface_hash.
- Graph request/response parsing.
- Delta token + subscription renewal logic.
- Error taxonomy mapping.
- Redaction.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic.
- Structured JSON logs.

Plus error taxonomy (401/403/404/429), FCP2 default-deny (3), lifecycle (4), auth mode tests (credential_id, missing perms, multiple auth), input validation (4). All 58 tests pass (29 inline + 29 integration), clippy clean, fmt clean.","created_at":"2026-03-02T10:40:52Z"}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-m9n","title":"fcp.airtable: Webhook automation for real-time sync","description":"# fcp.airtable: Webhook Automation for Real-Time Sync

## Goal
Support real-time Airtable change ingestion as an FCP2 streaming source.

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints`.
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Scope
- Webhook lifecycle:
  - `airtable.webhook.create`
  - `airtable.webhook.list`
  - `airtable.webhook.delete`
  - optional payload history APIs
- Event emission:
  - translate webhook payloads into EventEnvelopes

## Key requirements
- Connector must NOT expose inbound ports.
  - `fcp-host` owns ingress and forwards verified webhook payloads over FCPC.
- Capability gated (`airtable.webhooks.manage` is Dangerous).
- State model:
  - persist subscription identifiers / cursors if required
  - singleton-writer lease for renewals/cursor advancement
- Security:
  - validate webhook authenticity if Airtable provides signatures/secrets
  - replay protection if the platform provides it

## Tests
- Deterministic webhook payload simulation.
- Deny-by-default scenarios (no token).
- E2E: verify events are emitted with correct taints/provenance.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Webhook ingestion is deterministic and safe.
- Events integrate with the shared compliance harness.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-mapp","title":"[IDEA-25.2] Unit tests for parameter selection","description":"## Coverage
- MTU boundary cases.
- Path type selection (direct vs derp).
- Override precedence.

## Logging
- JSON logs with chosen parameters.
## Logging
- Emit structured JSONL per `docs/STANDARD_Testing_Logging.md` with `test_name`, `module`, `phase`, `correlation_id`, and `result`.
- Validate with `fcp_conformance::schemas::validate_e2e_log_jsonl` (use `fcp-testkit` LogCapture in unit tests).
","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add canonicalization proof tests: permute fields -> canonical bytes identical.
- Add deterministic vector generator with fixed seeds and algebraic edge cases.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Batch ed25519 verification and reuse hashers where safe.
- Avoid allocations in canonicalization using stack buffers.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-mbe","title":"fcp.kubernetes: Event watching and subscription system","description":"# fcp.kubernetes: Event watching and subscription system (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide streaming access to Kubernetes watch APIs and events so agents can:
- monitor resource changes
- detect failures and rollouts
- build alerting pipelines

## Scope
- Watch resources (pods/deployments/services) with label/namespace filters.
- Watch cluster events (bounded).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `k8s.watch` (sensitive)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Streaming must be bounded/backpressured:
  - cap queue depth
  - explicit drop/deny reason codes when overloaded
- Taint:
  - event payloads are tainted external input

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `k8s.watch.resource`
  - `k8s.watch.events`
- Event envelopes with stable event IDs and correlation IDs.

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - watch stream parsing
  - reconnect behavior
  - bounds/backpressure

## Acceptance criteria
- Watch streams are reliable and never cause unbounded memory growth.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-mfu0","title":"[IDEA-02.2] RateLimitDeclarations: SDK helpers + runtime status","description":"## Goal
Expose rate limit declarations and runtime status in `fcp-sdk`.

## Details
- Add SDK helpers to map manifest `rate_limits` into `OperationInfo` / `Introspection`.
- Add `RateLimitStatus` reporting utilities and a standard error mapping for limit violations.
- Ensure retry-after hints are exposed for host/CLI.

## Tests
- Unit tests validating SDK mapping and error taxonomy.
- Integration tests with a test connector that simulates throttling.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Connectors can advertise and report limits with minimal boilerplate.
- Rate limit violations are deterministic and include structured retry metadata.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add metamorphic tests (encode->decode->encode) plus differential oracle for edge cases.
- Use sequential test budgets with e-value thresholds to stop early when safe.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Parallelize vector generation with deterministic seeds.
- Cache canonical forms to avoid repeated parse cost.
